title: To Catch a Fish
description: <p>To Catch a Fish presents over sixty works of craft and art that are either used in fishing or that illuminate the relationship between people and fish. Utilitarian fishing objects include hand-crafted basketry fish traps, scrapers, weights, and nets. Works with spiritual significance and symbolism include amulets, robes, images of saints, and even kites. This <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/to-catch-a-fish/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n

title: 60th Anniversary "Diamond Gala"
description: As Autism Society San Diego celebrates its 60th Anniversary, on October 3, 2026, the Diamond Gala represents a milestone moment to honor six decades of inclusion and community impact. This event is designed not only as a celebration to honor our past, celebrate our present and shape our future, but as a strategic opportunity to deepen relationships with corporate partners, expand mission-aligned engagement, and secure critical funding for programs that directly support autistic individuals and their families across San Diego County.

title: San Diego Padres vs. Atlanta Braves
description: Monday, June 22, 2026 - First Pitch 7:10PM Promotions: Western Snapback Hat Presented by Petco Dial in your gameday look with a crisp Padres cap that completes any outfit. The Western Snapback Hat, presented by Petco, is limited to the first 40,000 fans in attendance and is a must have for all Padres fans. More Info Tuesday, June 23, 2026 - First Pitch 6:40PM Promotions: First Responder Salute to Law Enforcement Presented by GOVX Join us for our First Responder Salute, presented by GOVX, as we celebrate, honor and thank first responders! Receive 25% off tickets on dedicated First Responder Salutes and 10% off other games, available online through verification by GOVX.com. More Info Wednesday, June 24, 2026 - First Pitch 5:40PM

title: San Diego Padres vs. Los Angeles Dodgers
description: Friday, June 26, 2026 - First Pitch 6:45PM Promotions: Party in the Park Presented by Southwest Airlines Enjoy $5 drink specials and live music at Party in the Park, presented by Southwest Airlines, from the time gates open until first pitch. More Info Every Friday home game, it's a Party in the Park, presented by Southwest Airlines! Starting from the time gates open until first pitch (approximately 4-6:30 PM), join us for a pregame happy hour in the fully transformed Gallagher Square where fans can enjoy drink specials starting at $5, live music, and other fun activities! Fans must purchase Friar Funds for discounted drinks. Friar Funds ($5 value) will be available for purchase in Gallagher Square during each Party in the Park and can also be used at food and beverage locations throughout Petco Park. Themes may vary from week to week, but it's ALWAYS a great time. Grab your friends, secure your tickets, and we'll see you there! Saturday, June 27, 2026 - First Pitch 5:40PM Sunday, June 28, 2026 - First Pitch 1:10PM Promotions: KidsFest Presented by Nuna Families are encouraged to arrive when gates open for KidsFest, presented by Nuna, to enjoy bounce houses and other inflatables, games, face painters, balloon artists and other kid-friendly activities in the newly renovated Gallagher Square. More Info Kids Run the Bases Presented by Chick-fil-A After the game, the fun keeps going with Kids Run the Bases, presented by Chick-fil-A! Kids ages 14 and under are able to participate. More Info

title: Julian 4th of July Parade
description: On the 4th of July, Julian honors our nation's birthday with a salute to the men and women who made our country great, both domestically and militarily. For more than twenty years, Julian residents, their friends and parade lovers from all over have lined up on the town's Main Street to wave their flags, cheer the marching bands, salute the Marine Color Guard, and carry a giant American Flag down the thoroughfare. We will be honoring all Julian Pie Companies as the Grand Marshal of the 2026 parade under the Theme: As American as Apple Pie Past dignitaries include: Sally Snipes, The Historical Society, Jim Mazzone, the first person to organize the Julian Parade; Other dignitaries include: Ramona Senior Center, Veterans from the American Legion; and Miss Julian and her court. Antique cars, vintage tractors, belly dancers, World War 2 Submariners, and several comedy entries will appear to complete a fun well rounded hometown parade. Schedule 9:00AM - Main Street in Julian closes to vehicles 9:25AM - Welcome by Jim Kealy - Chaplain of the American legion Post 468 9:30AM - Pie-eating contest signups begin at the Main Stage 9:35AM - History with George Washington and Patrick Henry 9:55AM - Welcome by California State Assembly member Carl DeMaio 10:00AM - Doves & Desperados Performance 10:25AM - Two Pie-eating contests: 5 adults and 5 kids. (Thank you, Julian Pie Company for donating the pies!) 10:50AM - Musical performance - San Diego Firefighters, Emerald Society Pipes & Drums, 525 Brass Band and Sidle Entertainment Drum-line Band 11:30AM - Anyone wishing to help carry the Flag down Main Street, please meet at the intersection of Main Street and C Street 11:35AM - Raffle drawing winner announcement 11:50AM - The National Anthem by Anndrea Retz 11:55AM - The Flag is carried down Main Street 12:00Noon - Enjoy watching a fly-over of vintage airplanes. The parade begins after the enormous American Flag has been carried, by the audience, down Main Street

title: An Old Fashioned 4th of July
description: California State Parks and the Boosters of Old Town invite you to step back in time and celebrate an old fashioned Fourth of July at Old Town San Diego State Historic Park! This family-friendly event offers a unique opportunity to experience Independence Day as early San Diego residents did in the 1800s, with a day full of games, crafts, live music, and more. Activities Flag Raising Ceremony: Kick off the festivities with a patriotic flag raising in the Plaza de Las Armas. Opening Remarks: Hear from park staff and special guests as they welcome visitors. Live Music: Enjoy performances at three different locations within the park: Plaza Stage Fiesta de Reyes Casa de Maria Demonstrations: Spinning/Weaving Community Quilt: Contribute to a shared quilting project. Blacksmith: Watch live demonstrations of blacksmithing techniques. Archaeology: Learn about the historical digs and finds in Old Town. Games for All Ages: Hoop Toss Coit Game of Graces Craft Activities: Corn Husk Cloth Dolls: Create your own traditional dolls. Morse Code Bracelets: Make and take home a personalized bracelet. Competitive Games: Watermelon Eating Contest Tug of War Sack Race Egg Race Wheelbarrow Races Marble Contest

title: Scoop San Diego
description: Scoop San Diego is a delightful annual event that attracts ice cream lovers from near and far. With an anticipated attendance of over 6,000 guests, this festival features a wide array of artisanal ice cream vendors showcasing their unique flavors and creations. Visitors can indulge in unlimited tastings, enjoy live music, participate in ice cream eating contests, and even take part in interactive workshops. Scoop San Diego promises a sweet escape for anyone with a love for frozen treats.

title: Dance Dance Evolution (TGIF Concerts in the Park)
description: Dance Dance Evolution (DDE) is a dynamic live dance band composed of experienced professional musicians who have performed alongside top artists and toured nationally. They deliver a wide range of music genres from the 1970s to the present day, including disco, funk, pop, rock, R&B, and modern dance music, all performed live.

title: SDBlackACD Music Festival
description: The SDBlackACD Music Festival is a family-friendly gathering that promises a day filled with music and fun. With no admission fee, it offers an affordable way to enjoy live performances and create lasting memories.

title: Harbor Party (TGIF Concerts in the Park)
description: Harbor Party, Los Angeles's top yacht rock band, delivers an energetic performance that celebrates the iconic hits of the '70s and '80s with exceptional musicianship and theatrical panache. Their lively shows not only captivate audiences but also amplify the musical legacies of artists like Steely Dan, Chaka Khan, Kenny Loggins, Toto, and Al Jarreau, who defined this era of popular music.

title: ABBAFAB: A Tribute to ABBA (Coronado Promenade Summer Concert Series)
description: ABBAFab is a stunning tribute to the music of ABBA, featuring all the band's greatest hits in a night of pure nostalgia. Attendees can expect a high-energy performance that will have them dancing and singing along to their favorite ABBA songs.

title: Sabrosas Latin Orchestra (TGIF Concerts in the Park)
description: Sabrosas Latin Orchestra, the first all-female salsa band in San Diego, is known for their high-energy performances in a variety of Latin styles, including salsa, cumbia, and cha cha. They aim to bring the joy of music and dance to all, while encouraging women to develop their musical talents for future generations.

title: '90s Rockshow (TGIF Concerts in the Park)
description: Immerse yourself in a nostalgic '90s evening, filled with flannel-clad fun and iconic grunge, alt-rock, and pop-punk tunes. Prepare to rock out to the timeless hits of bands like Nirvana, Alanis Morissette, Pearl Jam, No Doubt, Red Hot Chili Peppers, Green Day, The Cranberries, REM, Alice In Chains, Smashing Pumpkins, Oasis, Stone Temple Pilots, Soundgarden, and more.

title: B.I.G. (TGIF Concerts in the Park)
description: B.I.G. (Brothers Igniting a Groove) is a Southern California-based band specializing in R&B, smooth jazz, and old school funk, with a repertoire of over 100 songs. Founded in 2002, the band has shared stages with numerous renowned artists and is committed to delivering top-notch performances every time.

title: Hillcrest CityFest
description: Get ready for San Diego’s largest and most vibrant arts and music festival at Hillcrest CityFest. This day-to-night celebration fills the streets with live music, DJ sets, street performers, and over 250 vendors offering everything from handcrafted art to delicious local eats. Known for its high-energy atmosphere and community spirit, CityFest is the perfect place to dance, shop, and celebrate the diversity and creativity of Hillcrest. Whether you’re a local or just visiting, this festival is a must-experience tribute to art, culture, and pride.

title: FlashPants (TGIF Concerts in the Park)
description: Get ready to ignite your inner rockstar with FlashPants, an electrifying tribute to the decade of outrageous style and unforgettable anthems. This is not your average synth-infused flashback, but a chance to grab your crew, forget (or don't forget) your leg warmers, and sing along like it's 1989.

title: World Bodysurfing Championships
description: The World Bodysurfing Championships bring together top bodysurfers from around the globe to compete at the iconic Oceanside Pier in San Diego, California. Founded in 1977, this event showcases the skill and athleticism of participants as they ride the waves using only their bodies. Spectators can enjoy thrilling heats, a lively beach atmosphere, and the chance to witness world-class talent in a unique and historic surf setting. The championships are a must-see for ocean lovers and those seeking an authentic Southern California experience.

title: Daring Greatly (TGIF Concerts in the Park)
description: Daring Greatly, a harmony-driven rock band from Canada, now based in Southern California, is known for their captivating blend of '70s rock, modern soul, folk, and country, along with their powerful three-part vocal harmonies and heartfelt songwriting. Attendees can expect an energetic live show from a band that has shared stages with acts like Bon Jovi and The Doobie Brothers, and continues to inspire fans with their bold and authentic music.

title: Adams Avenue Street Fair
description: The Adams Avenue Street Fair in San Diego is a dynamic celebration of music and local culture. As Southern California’s largest free music festival, it features live performances across multiple stages, unique vendors, and delicious food offerings. With a laid-back atmosphere, it’s a must-attend event for music lovers and festival-goers alike.

title: San Diego International Dragon Boat Race
description: Unleash the dragons at the San Diego International Dragon Boat Race, where the race competition is fierce, and there are pre-race festivities such as lion dance, music, and performances, as well as a Vendor Village with food trucks, jewelry, clothing, and more. If you love a fiery competition, then row over to the San Diego International Dragon Boat Race.

title: STRENGTH IN THE CITY Festival
description: This health and wellness festival is a community-driven event that celebrates movement, mindfulness, and local connections, presented by Atlanta Life Insurance Company. Attendees can look forward to exploring over 20 mini-classes, including yoga, pilates, HIIT, dance, meditation, and equipment-based workouts, along with recovery experiences, local vendors, food, and fun.

title: Red, White & BOOM! at LEGOLAND® California
description: FIREWORKS • DANCE PARTIES • MEET & GREETS Summer heats up at LEGOLAND® California Resort with a star-spangled 4th of July celebration: Red, White & BOOM! Families can enjoy special limited-time treats, fun lawn activities, and DJ dance parties that will have everyone on their feet. End the night with an unforgettable fireworks display that explodes into 3D LEGO® bricks right before your eyes with special viewing glasses! Special extended park hours to celebrate and enjoy the fireworks spectacular Themed entertainment throughout the Resort PLUS, all Red, White, & BOOM! activities are included in the cost of regular Park admission *Special firework viewing glasses available while supplies last. Awesome Activities Shows including the Red, White & BOOM! Bash and Race Car Guy's Super-Fast Dance Party. It's an awesome way to CELE-BRICK! MINILAND Scavenger Hunt with an EXCLUSIVE Pop Badge prize Lawn games hosted by Hot Dog Guy including LEGO Hamburger Relay, character meet & greets+ MORE!​ And, of course, an AWESOME firework show with pre-firework entertainment!

title: Tanabata Festival
description: The Tanabata Festival celebrates the ancient tale of star-crossed lovers Orihime and Hikoboshi, who reunite just once a year across the Milky Way. This vibrant summer event invites guests of all ages to enjoy games, performances, crafts, and traditional foods in a lively and family-friendly atmosphere. Rooted in Japanese folklore, the festival blends cultural celebration with seasonal fun.

title: Disney's Beauty and The Beast The Musical
description: Be Our Guest at BEAUTY AND THE BEAST, Disney's first North American touring production of the beloved musical in over 25 years. This enchanting and timeless tale has been brought to life like never before, with spectacular new sets and dazzling costumes. The show boasts the Oscar®-winning and Tony Award®-nominated score, including the classic songs "Be Our Guest" and "Beauty and the Beast." Reuniting to create this new production are members of the original Tony Award®-winning artistic team, including composer Alan Menken, lyricist Tim Rice, book writer Linda Woolverton, with direction and choreography by Matt West, scenic design by Stanley A. Meyer, costume design by Ann Hould-Ward and lighting design by Natasha Katz. BEAUTY AND THE BEAST is the beloved musical that will fill your heart with joy and Disney magic.

title: Spirit of Stonewall Rally
description: Join the vibrant LGBTQ+ community and allies at the annual Spirit of Stonewall Rally in San Diego, California. Commemorating the historic Stonewall Riots, this event features powerful speakers, live music, and a colorful parade, drawing in an anticipated attendance of over 30,000 attendees. Embrace the spirit of love, acceptance, and equality at this inspirational celebration.

title: Pride of Hillcrest
description: Kick off San Diego Pride with Pride of Hillcrest, a high-energy block party and celebration in the heart of the city’s most vibrant LGBTQ+ neighborhood. This electrifying event features live performances, DJs, craft cocktails, and a dazzling light show, setting the stage for an unforgettable weekend of pride and unity. Dance under the stars, connect with the community, and celebrate love, diversity, and inclusion in one of the most welcoming and dynamic Pride events on the West Coast.

title: Hell's Kitchen
description: Ali is a 17-year-old girl full of fire - searching for freedom, passion and her place in the world. How she finds them is a New York City coming-of-age story you've never felt before - HELL'S KITCHEN, a new musical from 16-time Grammy® Award winner Alicia Keys, whose new songs and greatest hits about growing up in NY inspire a story made for Broadway. Rebellious and stifled by an overprotective single mother, Ali is lost until she meets her mentor: a neighbor who opens her heart and mind to the power of the piano. Set to the rhythm of the 90s, HELL'S KITCHEN is a love story between a mother and daughter. It's about finding yourself, your purpose, and the community that lifts you. Come remember where dreams begin. Hailed as a New York Times Critic's Pick that is, "thrilling from beginning to end - a rare must-see!" HELL'S KITCHEN is directed by four-time Tony Award nominee Michael Greif, with choreography by four-time Tony Award nominee Camille A. Brown, a book by Pulitzer Prize finalist Kristoffer Diaz, and the music of Alicia Keys.

title: LEGO® Festival at LEGOLAND® California
description: Imagine a festival built especially for kids, where play takes center stage! LEGO® Festival returns to LEGOLAND® Resorts in 2026, with all-new exciting headliners and returning fan favorites in the Music, Creativity, and Chill Out play zones - each packed with awesome family fun including: Brick build activities Jaw-dropping LEGO models Live shows Superstar LEGO characters ready to make memories with you! And the best part? On top of all the LEGO Festival excitement, you can still enjoy every ride, attraction, and show that makes LEGOLAND so unforgettable. It's the ultimate celebration of play - only at LEGOLAND this summer.

title: National University Bill Walton Classic
description: Sports San Diego officials have announced a new college basketball event, the National University Bill Walton Classic, featuring a women's game and a men's game at Pechanga Arena on Saturday, November 7. The women's matchup features UC San Diego vs. the University of San Diego, followed by the men's matchup featuring San Diego State University vs. an opponent to be announced at a later date. Official game times will be announced at a later date. Tickets are available at www.billwaltonclassic.com.

title: Studio Arts Workshop: Animal Paintings
description: Join us for Studio Arts, when OMA transforms into an artist's studio, offering skill-building workshops led by distinguished professor and arts educator Robin Douglas. Explore media, techniques, & creativity in a supportive, skill-building environment. Animal Paintings This is an especially fun one! Bring in photographs of your favorite furry beasts and draw and paint them in a unique background inspired by famous paintings of animals at this art workshop. All supplies and light refreshments for each workshop are included in the cost. A break for lunch and relaxed discussion will occur midday. Please bring your own bagged lunch or feel free to visit one of the restaurants close by on our provided list. Ticket Prices: Adult: $100 Member: $70 Space is limited. Register today!

title: Artist Alliance Journaling For Creatives: How to Understand Your Story
description: In this virtual workshop series, artists are invited to turn their journal into a studio companion and idea incubator. Through interactive Zoom sessions we'll explore simple, repeatable practices artists can use to generate content, clarify ideas, and deepen connection to their work. NOVEMBER TOPIC: HOW TO UNDERSTAND YOUR STORY In this interactive journaling workshop, you'll: - Explore your core values and creative motivations -Use guided journaling prompts to uncover your personal "Why" -Shape words that inspire your art, fuel your career, and connect you with audiences You will receive a Zoom link in the emailed RSVP confirmation receipt. Ticket Prices: Artist Alliance Member: Free

title: November: Artist Alliance Quarterly Critique
description: Artist Alliance members, along with their guests, are welcome to engage in a group art share and critique, aimed at fostering professional growth. Not yet part of the alliance? No worries, you can join either before the event or on-site! Ticket Prices: Artist Alliance Member: Free

title: Taste of Art Workshop: Collage-Layers of Meaning and Textures
description: Kick off your weekend with a bite-sized happy hour version of our renowned Studio Arts workshops. Gather with friends in an artistic space designed to amplify your creative side. Join arts educator Robin Douglas, as we dip our toes into the work of celebrated artists over drinks and nibbles, followed by a rapid demonstration of their styles. You'll then be guided to interpret these artists and create your own singular, spectacular work of art. We'll help you express your unique style and ensure you have a blast doing it. Collage-Layers of Meaning and Textures Using rich papers and photo images, text, and color media, you will create an original thought-provoking masterpiece at this workshop. Different presentation techniques will be explored. A fantastic skill-building opportunity! All levels of experience are encouraged to participate. OMA provides food, drinks and supplies; all you have to do is get yourself out of work, grab your friends, and have fun! Ticket Prices: Adult: $65 Member: $50 Space is limited. Register today!

title: Plein Air Paint Out With OMA Artist Alliance: Oceanside Pier
description: Come paint the Oceanside Pier at the end of Pier View Way or the surrounding Oceanside beach shops and coastline! We'll meet at the start of the Pier at 2:00pm and fan out from there. Free parking is available at the lot at the corner of Seagaze and S Cleveland Drives or metered parking can be found immediately adjacent to the Pier. Public restrooms are available. Open to All Levels! Whether you're a seasoned plein air painter or just starting your artistic journey, this event is for you! Beginners and experienced painters can find fresh inspiration in the urban and natural landscape. How to Participate: Register for the event on the San Diego Plein Air Painters Meetup Group site: https://www.meetup.com/painter-86/ Bring your painting supplies, easel, and canvases. Don't forget sunscreen and a hat for the California sun! This is a non-instructional event. Paint to your heart's content and enjoy the dynamic surroundings. Become an OMA Artist Alliance Member: Join and learn more about Artist Alliance and the activities at Oceanside Museum of Art at: https://oma-online.org/membership/ Ticket Prices: Artist Alliance Member: Free

title: Artist Alliance Monthly Social: Hangar 76
description: Hangar 76 OMA Artist Alliance members and their guests are invited to gather each month for a lively social meetup hosted in San Diego and North County. These informal get-togethers offer a relaxed opportunity to connect with fellow artists, share ideas, and build community while exploring a range of hotspots across the region. Whether you're catching up with familiar faces or meeting someone new, each gathering encourages conversation, collaboration, and camaraderie within the Artist Alliance network. This is a no-host event, attendees are welcome to purchase their own food and beverages. Ticket Prices: Artist Alliance Member: Free

title: Exhibition Celebration: December 2026
description: Join us for an evening of art and drinks as we celebrate the opening of our newest exhibitions: Rupinder Kaloti: The Gathering Kaloust Guedel: Where Time Bends Softly Yvette Gellis: Toward Light and the Living Landscape Discover and connect with fellow artists, dedicated curators, and a vibrant community as you explore the art and stories of Southern California artists in three new exhibitions at OMA.

title: Art For All
description: Join OMA's Free First Sundays for Art for All! Each month, OMA welcomes friends and neighbors on Free First Sundays to join in Art for All, our fun hands-on art-making experience led by artists and cultural partners from our community. Whether you're new to art or have your own creative flow, Art for All is the time to let your imagination soar. Visit our current exhibitions to spark your creativity! Explore different art-making materials through creative projects at each session while learning from local and regional artists. Check back each month to see what we're up to next! These informal drop-in workshops are open to all ages. Children under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an adult. All materials are provided free of charge, while supplies last. All levels welcome! Ticket Prices: Free

title: "Keep Del Mar Green" Community Cleanups
description: DMVA invites you to take part in our "Keep Del Mar Green" community cleanup events! Together, we'll protect our beautiful coastline, support sustainability, and make a lasting impact on our environment. We'll meet at the 17th Street Del Mar Lifeguard Station before spreading out across the beach, parks, and nearby streets to collect litter that would otherwise pollute our precious waterways. To make your efforts even more rewarding, the Del Mar Lifeguards will work with us to treat our volunteers to a delicious breakfast of eggs and pancakes. During the cleanup, we'll use baskets from the Del Mar ShoreSaver Program—a pioneering environmental initiative led by DMVA in partnership with the City of Del Mar, EDCO, and Relic Coastal Collaborative—designed to keep our beaches pristine while promoting ecological health. We can't wait to see you there—let's keep Del Mar green! 2026-2027 community cleanup dates: Saturday, Sept. 19, 2026 Saturday, February 20, 2027 Saturday, April 17, 2027

title: Deep Sea
description: <p>Directed by San Diego resident and world-renowned underwater cinematographer Howard Hall, Deep Sea will transport audiences deep below the ocean surface to swim with some of the most exotic creatures on the planet, from the bizarre Rainbow Nudibranch to the Giant Pacific Octopus. Complementing the educational mission of the Fleet, Deep Sea will inspire audiences, not only with breathtaking <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/deep-sea/2026-05-14/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n

title: Kim’s Convenience
description: <p>Heart and humor take center stage in this award-winning comedy that inspired the hit Netflix series. Korean-born Mr. Kim runs a Toronto convenience store while raising his Canadian children with traditional values. As the neighborhood gentrifies and generational tensions rise, one family’s everyday struggles become a metaphor for a changing society. Written by and starring <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/kims-convenience/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n

description: <p>Heart and humor take center stage in this award-winning comedy that inspired the hit Netflix series. Korean-born Mr. Kim runs a Toronto convenience store while raising his Canadian children with traditional values. As the neighborhood gentrifies and generational tensions rise, one family’s everyday struggles become a metaphor for a changing society. Written by and starring <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/kims-convenience-2/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n

title: Artwalk at Liberty Station
description: Step into the world of creativity at ArtWalk Liberty Station, a vibrant festival that invites attendees to immerse themselves in the art community. Featuring over 175 artists from across the U.S. and Mexico, this event showcases a range of artistic mediums, including paintings, photography, sculpture, jewelry, and glass and ceramic art. Celebrating both local and international talent, ArtWalk highlights the cultural richness of the region, offering visitors the chance to discover and acquire original works of art in a dynamic, outdoor setting. It’s the perfect place to connect with artists and explore the creative pulse of San Diego.

title: Tiki Oasis
description: Experience the ultimate retro tiki lifestyle at Tiki Oasis, the largest and longest-running tiki festival in the world. With a vibrant mix of live music, dance performances, art exhibits, and cocktail tastings, this event draws in over 4,000 attendees each year. Immerse yourself in the colorful world of tiki culture and enjoy a weekend filled with Polynesian-inspired fun and entertainment.

title: San Diego Bayfair
description: The San Diego Bayfair is a thrilling celebration of powerboat racing, community, and waterfront fun, held annually on Mission Bay in San Diego, California. Known as one of the largest powerboat racing events in the United States, Bayfair features a weekend packed with hydroplane races, live entertainment, and family-friendly activities. Attendees can enjoy prime views of high-speed races, explore food and drink vendors, and relax in the scenic beauty of Mission Bay Park.

title: Mission Bayfest
description: Mission Bayfest brings top-tier live music to the heart of San Diego’s Mission Beach, set against the backdrop of Mariner’s Point Park’s sweeping bay views and lush open spaces. Festivalgoers can enjoy performances from acclaimed artists while relaxing on the grass or taking in the coastal scenery. The event’s unique waterfront setting creates a laid-back, unforgettable atmosphere that draws music fans from across the region. Mission Bayfest is a standout destination for those seeking a memorable festival experience by the water.

title: Forging a Legacy: 15 Years of Landmark Acquisitions
description: <p>This exhibition marks the centennial of The San Diego Museum of Art, celebrating a select few of the many thousands of important works of art added to the collection in its recent history. Since the Museum first opened its doors to the public in 1926 as the Fine Arts Gallery of San Diego, it has <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/forging-a-legacy-15-years-of-landmark-acquisitions/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n

title: India Thompson: Looks Like Home
description: <p>India Thompson reflects on ownership and emotional attachment through intricately woven reed sculptures, transforming everyday domestic objects into meditations on home in the age of renting. India Thompson: Looks Like Home explores emotional attachment to the ordinary domestic objects in the places we call home in the age of renting. By meticulously weaving works from reed, <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/india-thompson-looks-like-home/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n

title: San Diego Food and Wine Festival
description: The San Diego Food and Wine Festival Grand Weekend is a coastal celebration featuring hundreds of wineries, renowned chefs, farmers, and fishermen. Attendees can look forward to a diverse range of gourmet experiences, from mega tasting events and workshops to film screenings and cocktail culture, all in the company of cross-border chefs, winemakers, innovators, and global leaders.

title: Old Fashioned Fourth of July Celebration
description: Old Fashioned Fourth offers a unique turn of the 20th century style celebration of the country's independence, complete with patriotic entertainment, free children's crafts, train displays, and old-fashioned games. Attendees can enjoy tasty treats and train rides for a small fee, and free shuttle services are provided for convenience.

title: Fourth of July Fireworks
description: The City of El Cajon is set to light up the night with a spectacular fireworks display this July 4th. Attendees can also enjoy a host of free activities throughout the day, including train rides, arts and crafts, family games, and a DJ set by 35 Productions with DJ Danny.

title: Coronado 4th of July Celebration
description: Experience a vibrant celebration of Independence Day in Coronado. The event promises a day filled with patriotic parades, live music, and a spectacular fireworks display to cap off the evening.

title: Independence Day Fireworks in Imperial Beach
description: Immerse yourself in a day of patriotic pride, celebrating 250 years of the American spirit, complete with a full lineup of festivities, including live music and a breathtaking fireworks display. Enjoy the festive atmosphere, connect with your community, and reflect on the nation's rich history and bright future, all while grooving to the tunes of IB Waves Band and the Marine Band San Diego.

title: Fourth of July Celebration
description: SeaWorld San Diego promises a 4th of July filled with festive activities, from dazzling fireworks displays to special themed shows. Attendees can also look forward to unique animal encounters, making the event a perfect blend of fun and learning.

title: Deep Sea
description: <p>Directed by San Diego resident and world-renowned underwater cinematographer Howard Hall, Deep Sea will transport audiences deep below the ocean surface to swim with some of the most exotic creatures on the planet, from the bizarre Rainbow Nudibranch to the Giant Pacific Octopus. Complementing the educational mission of the Fleet, Deep Sea will inspire audiences, not only with breathtaking <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/deep-sea/2026-05-21/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n

title: San Marcos 4th of July Celebration
description: The City of San Marcos is set to host a lively Fourth of July celebration, brimming with spirited lawn games and jumpers. Attendees can look forward to an array of delectable offerings from food trucks and vendors, and are encouraged to bring beach chairs or blankets for comfortable lawn seating.

title: Escondido Independence Day Festival
description: The 61st Annual Independence Day Festival is a cherished community tradition that promises a day filled with music, culture, and patriotic fun. This free, family-friendly event is sure to delight attendees of all ages.

title: 4th of July Cruises On The Bay
description: This Fourth of July, celebrate America's 250th on the water where San Diego's story began. Catch the Big Bay Boom up close aboard a luxury yacht with open-air decks, live music, and skyline views that can't be beat. The whole weekend is packed with ways to enjoy the bay from happy hour cruises, scenic brunches, high-speed adventures, and ferry service to Coronado. Whether you're here for the fireworks or the full holiday weekend experience, there's a cruise for every kind of celebration. Join us for your most unforgettable Fourth yet.

title: 9th ANNUAL PROUD+ at The Studio Door
description: June 19-July 31, 2026Artist Reception: Saturday, July 11, 2026 • 6-9 PM A national exhibition of contemporary LGBTQ+ visual art, PROUD+ returns for its eighth year with works by leading artists from across the country. The exhibition reflects a high level of craft and professional distinction, with a special spotlight on local artist Drub. Held in conjunction with San Diego Pride Month (July). The reception is a major cultural event during the summer season, drawing visitors seeking bold, inclusive, and compelling art experiences. Mature themes. Recognized as San Diego's premier contemporary art gallery, The Studio Door presents rotating exhibitions throughout the year that reflect the breadth and vitality of today's visual arts. Showcasing artists from Southern California and across the nation, the gallery serves the full spectrum of contemporary practice from emerging voices to established professionals while fostering meaningful connections between artists, collectors, and the community. For visitors, The Studio Door offers an authentic cultural experience rooted in creative excellence and local character. Gallery Hours: Tuesday-Saturday, 11 AM-6 PMLocation: 3867 Fourth Avenue, San Diego, CA 92103Neighborhood: Hillcrest LGBTQ Cultural DistrictWebsite: www.thestudiodoor.com Social Media: @TheStudioDoorPhone: 619-255-2263

title: Ryanhood in Concert
description: Tucson-based acoustic duo Ryanhood have spent over two decades building a reputation as one of the most dynamic live acts on the touring circuit. Winners of the International Acoustic Music Awards for Best Group/Duo, Cameron Hood and Ryan Green got their start busking at Boston's Quincy Market, where a college booking agent discovered them and launched them into a touring career that now spans more than 1,500 shows across 47 states. Along the way they've shared stages with Jason Mraz, Matt Nathanson, and Train. Ryanhood's sound is built on virtuosic acoustic guitar interplay and turn-on-a-dime vocal harmonies forged in a decades-long friendship. Their performances move seamlessly between energetic, hopeful, and quietly moving — driven by rich storytelling, inquisitive lyrics, and big acoustic riffs that blur the line between folk and rock. Back home in Tucson, they've won more than a dozen Tucson Music Awards including both Best Folk Band and Best Rock Band.

title: Tacos & Tequila: RBI Select Release Party
description: Join us on National Tequila Day Friday, July 24th, for an unforgettable evening at Buena Vista Terrace as we celebrate the debut of our exclusive RBI Select Barrel of Maestro Dobel. This spirited outdoor gathering brings together refined taste and vibrant energy, featuring guided tequila tastings, artfully crafted cocktails, and a gourmet taco experience. Savor hand-pressed corn tortillas with freshly carved tacos al pastor, grilled fish tacos, and a selection of tequila-infused salsas and fresh guacamole—each element thoughtfully designed to complement the character and complexity of the evening's featured tequila.

title: Andy McKee in Concert
description: Andy McKee is a world-renowned guitarist from Kansas, best known for his original compositions for steel-string acoustic guitar. After discovering the music of modern fingerstyle players like Michael Hedges, Preston Reed, and Don Ross as a teenager, McKee turned away from the electric guitar and devoted himself to exploring the possibilities of the acoustic, using altered tunings, tapping, partial capos, and percussive techniques to transform a single instrument into a full orchestra. In 2006, his original composition "Drifting" became one of the first viral videos on YouTube, and several other pieces including "Rylynn," "For My Father," and "Into the Ocean" soon followed. To date, his videos have accumulated hundreds of millions of views and inspired a new generation of fingerstyle guitarists worldwide. Artists like John Petrucci, Prince, Eric Johnson, Steve Lukather, and David Foster have recognized his talent, recording or touring with him over the years. Beyond performing in over 40 countries as a soloist, McKee founded the Musicarium in 2015, a premier fingerstyle guitar camp that has featured guest instructors including Tommy Emmanuel, Eric Johnson, Stanley Jordan, and Don Ross. In 2024 he joined forces with Trevor Gordon Hall and Calum Graham to release tripliciti, an acclaimed acoustic guitar trio project. 2025 brings a new solo guitar album and a 20th anniversary re-recording of Art of Motion.

title: California State Summer Games
description: This event is designed to foster health, education, and competitive spirit among Californians through a top-notch amateur sports competition. It encourages participation and aims to provide an experience akin to the Olympics.

title: Little Shop of Horrors feat. Ellen Greene
description: Celebrate the 40th Anniversary of Little Shop of Horrors with a screening hosted by the original Audrey, Ellen Greene! This is a rare appearance by Ellen Greene, who originated the role in the stage musical and continued on to star in everyone's favorite film adaptation. VIP tickets includes the best seats and pre-show meet & greet with Ellen Greene including a piece of signed memorabilia and photo-ops!Little Shop of Horrors is the 1986 cult-classic sci-fi musical comedy directed by Frank Oz, featuring a nerdy florist, Seymour Krelborn, who cultivates a man-eating, blood-thirsty plant named Audrey II. As the plant brings fame to his Skid Row shop, it demands increasingly human sacrifices, forcing Seymour to balance his love for coworker Audrey with the plant's deadly appetite.

title: The Rocky Horror Picture Show feat. Patricia Quinn
description: ORIGINAL CAST SPOOKTACULAR TOUR!Join the original 'Magenta' - PATRICIA QUINN - for a screening of the movie in a new 4k restoration with a live shadow cast and audience participation! Plus a costume contest and more! *Get exclusive tickets to the V.I.P. Meet & Greet to meet Patricia Quinn herself, have a personal photo opportunity, & get an autograph.$100 V.I.P. MEET & GREET INCLUDES VIP Meet & Greet Seating Section - The best seats in the house! Special Rocky Horror VIP Laminate Get a photo taken with Patricia Quinn with your own camera Bring your own personal item to autograph, or pick out a photo from Patricia's collection to get signed No late admittance after 7pm.

title: TUSK: The Classic Tribute to Fleetwood Mac
description: Playing the hits and deep cuts spanning the careers of Rock N Roll Hall of Fame inductees Fleetwood Mac, Tusk has been crisscrossing the country since 2008 as The Classic Fleetwood Mac Tribute. Five pro musicians turned into masters of their trade through decades of individual music study, live performance, creative risk, and devotion to the art. TUSK features note-perfect renditions of both the solo and full band songs, with each member specializing in one of the core lineup of Fleetwood Mac (Lindsey Buckingham, Stevie Nicks, Christine McVie, John McVie, and Mick Fleetwood). They possess an electric stage presence that can only come from well over a decade touring together. Truly the best at what they do, TUSK is unmatched in their tribute to one of the greatest bands of all time.

title: Big Bad Voodoo Daddy's Wild And Swingin' Holiday Party
description: For 32 years, Big Bad Voodoo Daddy's unique take on American swing and jazz music has thrilled audiences around the world, while their unique and spirited "Wild and Swingin' Holiday Party" has become an eagerly anticipated annual family event. Singer and band leader Scotty Morris says, "If you're going to go to a holiday show with your family, this is the one! It really does offer something for everyone." Drawing on a rich catalogue of holiday classics and Christmas originals from the band's two full length holiday albums, Big Bad Voodoo Daddy brings its world-renowned live show, and fun and quirky take on the holidays, to you. Fun arrangements of classic holiday songs in Big Bad Voodoo Daddy style include: Jingle Bells, Santa Claus Is Coming to Town, and Winter Wonderland. Rudolph the Red Nosed Reindeer is "Andrew's Sisters" meets the blues, and the just as classic Heat Miser song transports listeners back to another beloved holiday special. The band rips through Chuck Berry's Run, Run Rudolph, Lou Rawls' Merry Christmas Baby, and slows things down for Elvis Presley's Blue Christmas. Detours to New Orleans for Frosty the Snowman, and to the Caribbean for the Calypso A Party for Santa, keep things interesting. Big Bad Voodoo Daddy has always made writing original music a priority, and the holidays are no exception. Rockabilly Christmas, Last Night (I Went Out With Santa Claus), Christmas Time in Tinsel Town, and the beautiful and reflective It Feels Like Christmas Time, are the band's take on the many feelings of the holidays. With three decades together, Big Bad Voodoo Daddy is still picking up steam and continues to tour, record, and entertain. Merry Christmas and Happy New Year!

title: Mariachi Sol de México de José Hernández A Merry-Achi Christmas
description: Latin Grammy nominees Maestro José Hernández and his world-renowned Mariachi Sol de México® - Embajadores del Mariachi - return with their annual Christmas holiday tour. The program showcases the ensemble's musicianship, playing mariachi favorites as well as the most well-known and beloved Christmas music such as Tchaikovsky's The Nutcracker Suite, performed mariachi-style by one of the most critically acclaimed Mariachis.

title: Virtual Art Share With OMA Artist Alliance: December
description: If you're looking for fellowship but can't make it in person to OMA, join our Virtual Art Share series from home or wherever you are with refreshments of your choice, and enjoy some conversation with fellow artists! Sculptors, painters, photographers, and artists of all disciplines are welcome. Take part in this conversation via a computer, tablet, or smartphone. How to Participate: Email your file (JPEG or PNG) to Theo, dehartt+artshare@gmail.com, with "OMA Virtual Art Share" in the subject line of your email. This online event is hosted on Zoom, join the meeting here. Meeting ID: 884 8509 1576 Content Advisory: This is a supportive peer group for artists of all experience levels, engaging with a broad range of subject matter, including but not limited to: artificial intelligence, anime, landscapes, various forms of sculpture, photography, portraiture, figurative work, LGBTQ themes, sexuality, politics, and war. Because the artwork and discussions may include mature or sensitive content, participants under the age of 18 must either be accompanied by a parent or guardian or have explicit guardian permission to attend. If any participant—adult or underage—prefers to avoid potentially triggering content, we are happy to accommodate by scheduling their presentation earlier in the session to allow for a timely and respectful departure.

title: Studio Arts Workshop: The Annual Figure Drawing and Painting Studio
description: Join us for Studio Arts, when OMA transforms into an artist's studio, offering skill-building workshops led by distinguished professor and arts educator Robin Douglas. Explore media, techniques, & creativity in a supportive, skill-building environment. The Annual Figure Drawing and Painting Studio Hone your skills and try new techniques to define the human form in this art workshop! You'll use different media, wet and dry, on both large and small-scale canvas and paper. Your creation could even be a great holiday gift for a loved one! All supplies and light refreshments for each workshop are included in the cost. A break for lunch and relaxed discussion will occur midday. Please bring your own bagged lunch or feel free to visit one of the restaurants close by on our provided list. Ticket Prices: Adult: $100 Member: $70 Space is limited. Register today!

title: Taste of Art Workshop: Gold Paintings
description: Kick off your weekend with a bite-sized happy hour version of our renowned Studio Arts workshops. Gather with friends in an artistic space designed to amplify your creative side. Join arts educator Robin Douglas, as we dip our toes into the work of celebrated artists over drinks and nibbles, followed by a rapid demonstration of their styles. You'll then be guided to interpret these artists and create your own singular, spectacular work of art. We'll help you express your unique style and ensure you have a blast doing it. Gold Paintings Go for the gold at this art workshop, where you will practice different ways to use this glowing pigment on canvas. Your work of art can be totally abstract with gold as the subject matter, or you can use metallic paint to elevate more traditional subjects. A great way to round out the year and channel your creative gifts! All levels of experience are encouraged to participate. OMA provides food, drinks and supplies; all you have to do is get yourself out of work, grab your friends, and have fun! Ticket Prices: Adult: $65 Member: $50 Space is limited. Register today!

title: Coffee and Conversation With OMA Artist Alliance: December
description: Relax over a steaming cup of coffee, and bring your artwork to share with your fellow Artist Alliance members! Each month one artist will have the opportunity to give a longer presentation, and practice sharing their work and ideas in front of a supportive group setting. This month's presenter has yet to be determined! *To be selected as a future presenter, please submit your name and website to cathy@artstudiosandiego.com. Ticket Prices: Artist Alliance Member: Free

title: Plein Air Paint Out With OMA Artist Alliance: The Star Theater
description: Paint the street view of the iconic Star Theater! As the day ends, stay to capture the iconic lights illuminating the building. Parking and restrooms are available at the nearby Civic Center. Open to All Levels! Whether you're a seasoned plein air painter or just starting your artistic journey, this event is for you! Beginners and experienced painters can find fresh inspiration in the urban and natural landscape. How to Participate: Register for the event on the San Diego Plein Air Painters Meetup Group site: https//www.meetup.com/painter-86/ Bring your painting supplies, easel, and canvases. Don't forget sunscreen and a hat for the California sun! This is a non-instructional event. Paint to your heart's content and enjoy the dynamic surroundings. Become an OMA Artist Alliance Member: Join and learn more about Artist Alliance and the activities at Oceanside Museum of Art at: https://oma-online.org/membership/ Ticket Prices: Artist Alliance Member: Free

title: Artist Alliance Journaling For Creatives: Drafting A Vision Statement
description: In this virtual workshop series, artists are invited to turn their journal into a studio companion and idea incubator. Through interactive Zoom sessions we'll explore simple, repeatable practices artists can use to generate content, clarify ideas, and deepen connection to their work. DECEMBER TOPIC: DRAFTING A VISION STATEMENT In this interactive journaling workshop, you'll: - Explore your core values and creative motivations -Use guided journaling prompts to uncover your personal "Why" -Shape words that inspire your art, fuel your career, and connect you with audiences You will receive a Zoom link in the emailed RSVP confirmation receipt. Ticket Prices: Artist Alliance Member: Free

title: Artist Alliance Monthly Social: Hangar 76
description: Hangar 76 OMA Artist Alliance members and their guests are invited to gather each month for a lively social meetup hosted in San Diego and North County. These informal get-togethers offer a relaxed opportunity to connect with fellow artists, share ideas, and build community while exploring a range of hotspots across the region. Whether you're catching up with familiar faces or meeting someone new, each gathering encourages conversation, collaboration, and camaraderie within the Artist Alliance network. This is a no-host event, attendees are welcome to purchase their own food and beverages. Ticket Prices: Artist Alliance Member: Free

title: Horse Power
description: <p>Journey across the globe in Horse Power, an epic, heartwarming story that celebrates the extraordinary bond between humans and horses and explores how these remarkable animals have shaped our history, <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/horse-power/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n

title: Aaron Hibell
description: Aaron Hibell is an electronic music artist from the UK known for blending atmospheric textures with intricate rhythms. His Synchronicity North American Tour 2026 showcases his debut album, SYNCHRONICITY, featuring immersive and emotionally resonant live performances. The tour follows his successful 2025 sold-out North America and Europe tour and highlights his ability to translate studio detail into a commanding live experience.

title: Derek Hough: Symphony of Dance Encore
description: Derek Hough presents Symphony of Dance: Encore, a dance performance event. The show is scheduled to take place at The Magnolia in El Cajon, California. Doors open at 7 PM with the show starting at 8 PM, and the event welcomes all ages.

title: Lou Deezi and DB.Boutabag
description: Lou Deezi and DB.Boutabag are performing together at Music Box in San Diego. The show starts at 8:00 PM with doors opening an hour earlier. This event is for ages 18 and up, offering a night of live music in a vibrant downtown venue.

title: San Diego Symphony Orchestra
description: The San Diego Symphony kicks off its Summer 2026 season at The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park with a program led by conductor Rafael Payare. Violinist Stefan Jackiw performs Prokofiev's Violin Concerto No. 2, showcasing his lyrical and rhythmic skill. The concert concludes with Tchaikovsky's Symphony No. 5, a powerful and expressive orchestral journey.

title: The Emo Night
description: Imagine a night filled with the best songs from Taking Back Sunday, Fall Out Boy, Panic at the Disco, and My Chemical Romance. The Emo Night Tour brings these iconic emo anthems to life all night long. It's a chance to relive the energy and emotion of your favorite tracks in a lively setting.

title: Laurel Canyon Revival
description: Laurel Canyon Revival is a five-member Southern California-based band composed of local music professionals. The band performs music specifically from the legendary Laurel Canyon music scene. The event features a live performance by this group.

title: Missing Persons
description: Missing Persons, a Los Angeles–based new wave band formed in 1980, continues to tour with lead singer Dale Bozzio performing their greatest hits. Bow Wow Wow, a new wave band known for its distinctive style and hits like “I Want Candy,” will appear as a special guest. The event features performances by both bands at Belly Up in Solana Beach, California.

title: The Beach Boys and San Diego Symphony Orchestra
description: The Beach Boys bring their iconic California sound to The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park, accompanied by the San Diego Symphony Orchestra. This concert celebrates 250 years of the USA and the 60th anniversary of their album Pet Sounds. Experience classic hits like "Surfin' USA" and "Good Vibrations" performed with orchestral arrangements in a unique summer setting.

title: Big Fish
description: Big Fish Summer 2026 is a musical adaptation of the novel by Daniel Wallace and the Tim Burton film, telling the story of Edward Bloom and his larger-than-life adventures. The production explores themes of storytelling, family bonds, and identity through a heartwarming narrative and memorable characters. You will experience a mix of humor and emotion in this theatrical journey presented by Trinity Theatre Company.

title: MrBallen
description: MrBallen is returning to the stage with new stories of nightmares. He is a prominent internet storyteller with a large following on YouTube and podcasts. This tour will feature tales of terror and suspense designed to captivate audiences.

title: Don Toliver
description: Don Toliver Octane Tour is a concert featuring American rapper, singer, and songwriter Don Toliver. The tour supports his fifth studio album OCTANE, released in 2026, which blends melodic trap and R&B with cinematic and immersive themes. The event includes performances by SahBabii, SoFaygo, and Chase B as supporting artists.

title: Les Misérables: School Edition
description: Les Misérables School Edition is set in nineteenth-century France and follows Jean Valjean, who is released from unjust imprisonment and struggles for redemption while being pursued by police inspector Javert. The story culminates during the Paris student uprising of 1832, where Javert confronts his ideals after Valjean spares his life and saves a student revolutionary. The production is a youth adaptation of the classic musical based on Victor Hugo's novel.

title: Trivia Night
description: Trivia Night at 207 is on! Grab your crew, test your knowledge, and enjoy great drinks and good vibes in the heart of the Gaslamp. We'll be having themed nights, so stay tuned.

title: Hopeful., communitypool, Porter Davis
description: Hopeful., communitypool, and Porter Davis come together for a night at Casbah in San Diego. The event starts with doors opening at 7:00 PM and the show beginning at 7:30 PM. It's a 21+ event, so bring your valid ID to enjoy the performances.

title: MicroMania Micro Athletes and Wrestling
description: MicroMania is a high-energy pro wrestling show featuring micro athletes under five feet tall who bring a mix of athleticism and comedy. The performers have appeared in various TV shows, movies, and events, making the show entertaining and unique. Expect to be engaged from start to finish as these athletes deliver a memorable wrestling experience.

title: PFL San Diego
description: The Professional Fighters League returns to Southern California with PFL San Diego Presented by GOVX on June 27, 2026. The event features a key featherweight bout between AJ McKee and Salamat Isbulaev. You can watch the main card live on ESPN 2 or catch the preliminaries on the ESPN app.

title: Natsu Nights
description: Natsu Nights is a summer night market series held inside the Japanese Friendship Garden and Museum. The garden transforms into a lively space with themed vendors, activities, and extended hours on select Saturdays from June through August. You can stroll the garden pathways, enjoy boba, and experience a unique outdoor atmosphere in the evening.

title: La Jolla Music Society SummerFest
description: Celebrating its 40th anniversary, SummerFest offers an exhilarating four-week musical extravaganza featuring over 100 world-class musicians and 21 concerts. Under the direction of Music Director Inon Barnatan, the festival explores music shaped by history through iconic masterpieces, intimate jazz sets, bold premieres, and inspired collaborations, ensuring every concert is a memorable event.

title: Poway Rodeo
description: The Poway Rodeo is a thrilling spectacle of rodeo events that not only showcases the talents of skilled riders but also fosters community spirit and creates unforgettable experiences. The event's website is a treasure trove of information, offering ticket details, FAQs, and special opportunities for kids, while also highlighting the invaluable contributions of volunteers, sponsors, and local businesses.

title: Ocean Paradise
description: <p>Uncover the secrets of paradise. May 22, 2026 through November 19, 2026 Thousands of miles from civilization lie the keys to safeguarding our planet—three untouched atolls that give us insight into human impacts on Earth. Join us on <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/ocean-paradise/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n
venue: San Diego Natural History Museum (The Nat)

title: Back from the Brink
description: <p>How do you save a species when time is working against you? May 22, 2026 through November 19, 2026 Back From the Brink tells the remarkable stories of three extraordinary animal species rescued from <a href="https://balboapark.org/event/back-from-the-brink/" class="more-link">...</a></p>\n
venue: San Diego Natural History Museum (The Nat)

title: Ocean Beach Street Fair and Chili Cook-Off
description: Tempt your taste buds at the Ocean Beach Street Fair and Chili Cook-Off in San Diego, where more than 50,000 visitors come together for a chili cook-off and continuous live music, as well as a variety of food and merchandise vendors, a community art project, and a local artisan showcase. If you love a cocktail by the beach, then cruise to the Ocean Beach Street Fair and Chili Cook-Off.

title: San Diego International Triathlon
description: The San Diego International Triathlon is a renowned USAT-sanctioned race, offering athletes a scenic course along San Diego’s waterfront. Participants take on a calm bay swim, a demanding bike ride up to Cabrillo National Monument, and a flat run with stunning harbor views. Open to both newcomers and seasoned triathletes, the event concludes with a lively finish-line celebration featuring beer and mimosa gardens. This triathlon is a highlight of the local sports calendar, drawing competitors from across the country.

title: Taste of Adams Avenue
description: Taste of Adams Avenue in San Diego, California, invites food enthusiasts to explore a diverse array of flavors from over 40 local restaurants, coffee shops, bakeries, pubs, and breweries along the historic Adams Avenue corridor. Attendees can stroll, bike, or hop on a complimentary trolley to sample offerings from beloved spots like Blind Lady Ale House, Lestat’s, and Mariposa Ice Cream. The event also highlights the neighborhood’s unique shops, making it a perfect outing for those eager to discover both culinary delights and local businesses. Tickets are available for purchase online or at participating venues, with secure pickup at the will-call booth on event day.

title: Santee Salutes
description: Santee Salutes is a beloved Independence Day celebration held each year in Santee, San Diego. This free, family-friendly event brings the community together with live music, delicious food trucks, patriotic ceremonies, and classic backyard games. The festivities culminate in a spectacular fireworks display, making it a memorable way for families and friends to honor the holiday. Santee Salutes offers a welcoming atmosphere for all ages to enjoy the spirit of July 4th.

title: 4th of July Celebration (Imperial Beach)
description: Celebrate the American spirit at the 4th of July Celebration in San Diego, California, where the community gathers for a day filled with music, fun, and patriotic pride. Enjoy live performances, offering a lively mix of favorites and patriotic tunes. The festive atmosphere brings neighbors and visitors together along the scenic Imperial Beach Pier, culminating in a breathtaking fireworks show over the ocean. This event is a memorable way to honor the nation’s history and connect with the local community.

title: Sun and Sea Festival
description: The Sun and Sea Festival in Imperial Beach, San Diego, California, is a lively celebration of the coastal lifestyle, featuring world-class sand sculpting, live entertainment, and a variety of local vendors. Families and friends gather to enjoy creative sand masterpieces, beachside activities, and the welcoming community atmosphere. With its focus on fun, art, and the beauty of the Pacific shoreline, this event captures the spirit of summer in Southern California. Whether you're a local or visiting, it's a must-see experience for anyone who loves the sun, sand, and sea.

title: Sip of Julian
description: Sip of Julian invites guests to explore Julian, California, through a self-guided tasting adventure featuring the region’s acclaimed wine, craft beer, hard cider, and cocktails. This event showcases local tasting rooms, each offering samples of their hand-crafted beverages paired with small bites. Attendees receive a commemorative glass and wine tote, and can take advantage of a convenient shuttle service connecting the participating venues. It’s a perfect way to experience Julian’s scenic charm, local flavors, and welcoming community spirit.

title: Cardiff Dog Days of Summer
description: Cardiff Dog Days of Summer is a beloved community festival in Encinitas, California, celebrating all things canine. This free event brings together over 100 dog-focused vendors, rescue groups, and pet adoption agencies, offering a lively mix of shopping, pet services, and adoption opportunities. Attendees can enjoy entertaining dog contests, live music, food trucks, and a ticketed libation lounge, making it a perfect outing for families and pet lovers. With a welcoming atmosphere and a tradition spanning over 20 years, it’s a must-visit summer gathering for the local community and their furry companions.

title: University Heights Summer in the Park
description: University Heights Summer in the Park brings the community together at Old Trolley Barn Park, San Diego, for a beloved series of free outdoor concerts. For over 25 years, neighbors and visitors have gathered on Friday evenings to enjoy performances by a variety of local bands. Attendees are encouraged to bring a picnic or sample food from nearby University Heights restaurants while relaxing on the grass. The event is a perfect way to connect with friends and family while experiencing live music in a welcoming neighborhood setting.

title: CVC San Diego Triathlon
description: Experience the thrill of the CVC San Diego Triathlon and 5K at Chula Vista Bayfront Park, where athletes of all levels can take on flat, fast courses along the scenic San Diego Bay. As part of the renowned San Diego Triathlon Series and the Triple Crown of Triathlon, this event is a highlight of the summer for both newcomers and seasoned racers. Enjoy the convenience of a single-venue setup with the start, transition, finish line, and beer garden all in one lively location. Celebrate your achievements post-race with friends and family while soaking in beautiful waterfront views and a festive atmosphere.

title: San Diego LGBTQ Film Festival
description: FilmOut San Diego LGBTQ+ Film Festival in San Diego, California, is a celebrated annual event dedicated to uplifting queer stories through film. Organized by a local nonprofit, the festival features a curated selection of international features, documentaries, and shorts that explore a wide range of LGBTQ+ experiences. Attendees can enjoy engaging panel discussions and Q&A sessions with filmmakers and actors, fostering meaningful community connections. The festival also extends its impact with monthly screenings and special marathons throughout the year across San Diego County.

title: San Diego International Kids' Film Festival
description: The San Diego International Kids' Film Festival in Carlsbad, California, celebrates global storytelling by presenting culturally diverse films created for children and youth. The festival encourages young filmmakers and highlights positive media and inspiring role models. Attendees can enjoy a unique multicultural experience while discovering new perspectives through film. This event is dedicated to blending entertainment with education, making it a must-visit for families and film enthusiasts alike.

title: Brazilian Festival
description: Experience the excitement of Brazilian Festival in San Diego, where the city’s scenic Waterfront Park transforms into a lively celebration of Brazil’s music, dance, and culinary traditions. This festival brings together communities to enjoy live performances from acclaimed Brazilian artists, interactive cultural exhibits, and authentic cuisine from local vendors. Families can look forward to dedicated kids’ activities and games, while shoppers will find unique crafts and products from artisans. Whether you’re drawn by the rhythms of samba or the flavors of Brazil, this event offers a welcoming atmosphere for all ages to connect and celebrate together.

title: Bayside Bands, Brew, and BBQ
description: Bayside Bands, Brew, and BBQ at SeaWorld San Diego invites guests to enjoy a lively blend of live music, savory barbecue, and a wide array of craft brews in a scenic waterfront setting. Savor expertly smoked and grilled dishes while sampling local and regional beers, all while taking in energetic performances from top musical acts. This event is perfect for families and friends looking to celebrate summer with great food, refreshing drinks, and unforgettable entertainment by the bay.

title: Cardiff Greek Festival
description: Experience the lively spirit of Greek culture at the Cardiff Greek Festival in San Diego, where the community gathers at Saints Constantine and Helen Greek Orthodox Church for a weekend of authentic food, traditional music, and spirited dancing. Children under 12, as well as first responders, veterans, and active military with ID, enjoy free admission. Please note that only credentialed service dogs are permitted on the festival grounds in accordance with county health regulations.

title: U.S. Open Adaptive Surfing Championships
description: The US Open Adaptive Surfing Championships at Oceanside Pier, San Diego, California, brings together elite adaptive surfers from around the world to compete in nine professional divisions for a substantial prize purse. Hosted by the Stoke for Life Foundation, this event is a highlight of the Adaptive Surfing Professionals World Championship Tour and celebrates the achievements of athletes with physical and visual impairments. Spectators can witness inspiring performances while supporting efforts to promote the therapeutic power of surfing and advocate for accessible beaches. The event has become a must-see for anyone interested in adaptive sports and inclusive community engagement.

title: Encinitas Oktoberfest
description: Encinitas Oktoberfest in San Diego, California, is a lively celebration of German heritage that brings the community together with authentic Bavarian music, dance performances, and a traditional beer garden. The event features a bustling artisan faire, showcasing local crafts and businesses, alongside an international food court and a dedicated craft beer garden highlighting regional brews. Family-friendly carnival attractions add to the festive atmosphere, making it a popular destination for attendees from across San Diego County. Spanning nearly a mile on Mountain Vista Drive, this annual festival offers something for everyone to enjoy.

title: Del Mar Harvest Festival
description: The Del Mar Harvest Festival in San Diego, California, is a celebrated indoor arts and crafts event held at the Del Mar Fairgrounds. Attendees can explore a curated marketplace featuring hundreds of skilled artisans offering handmade jewelry, unique home decor, clothing, and original artwork. The festival also includes live entertainment and a variety of specialty foods, creating a lively atmosphere for families and shoppers alike. This event is a must-visit for those seeking one-of-a-kind finds and a festive community experience.

title: Escondido Grand Avenue Festival
description: The Escondido Grand Avenue Festival in San Diego, California, transforms Historic Downtown Escondido into a lively celebration with more than 300 vendors offering unique crafts, goods, and international cuisine. Visitors can enjoy live music and entertainment throughout the day, making it a popular destination for families and friends. The festival is known for its welcoming atmosphere and diverse selection of food and shopping, drawing crowds from across the region each year.

title: San Diego International Film Festival
description: Dubbed the largest film event in the region, the San Diego International Film Festival attracts national and international celebrities each year. The festival features over 120 of the best films to screen before they are officially released, as well as independent filmmaking and glamorous parties with filmmakers and actors. Engage in intriguing after-screening discussions that provide a deep understanding of the creative process with over 20,000 other film fanatics during the San Diego International Film Festival.

title: Bike the Coast
description: Bike the Coast in San Diego, California, invites cyclists of all ages and abilities to experience stunning ocean views along historic Highway 101 and the San Luis Rey River Trail. Riders can choose from a variety of distances, from 7 to 100 miles, passing through scenic coastal towns like Oceanside, Carlsbad, Encinitas, Solana Beach, and Del Mar. The event culminates with a lively Finish Festival on the waterfront in Oceanside, featuring food and celebratory drinks for participants. Whether you’re a seasoned cyclist or a family looking for a fun day out, Bike the Coast offers a memorable way to enjoy Southern California’s coastline.

title: San Diego Zoo Halgloween
description: San Diego Zoo Halgloween transforms the iconic San Diego Zoo into a dazzling nighttime celebration filled with glowing lights and festive Halloween fun. Families can explore the zoo after dark, enjoying interactive activities, themed entertainment, and whimsical surprises around every corner. This annual event offers a unique way to experience the zoo, blending the magic of wildlife with the excitement of Halloween in a safe, family-friendly atmosphere. Perfect for all ages, Halgloween promises memorable moments and plenty of seasonal cheer.

title: Old Globe Día de los Muertos (Day of the Dead Celebration)
description: The Old Globe Día de los Muertos (Day of the Dead Celebration) in San Diego, California, invites the community to honor loved ones through a lively blend of music, dance, and traditional altars. Hosted at the iconic Old Globe, this event features performances, art workshops, and opportunities to learn about the rich cultural significance of Día de los Muertos. Families and visitors of all ages can participate in hands-on activities and enjoy colorful displays that celebrate life and remembrance. This annual gathering fosters connection and cultural appreciation in a welcoming, festive atmosphere.

title: San Diego Bay Wine and Food Festival
description: Tantalize your taste buds at the San Diego Bay Wine and Food Festival, dubbed the best general food festival in the country. The festival features a thriving food scene where the city’s most drool-worthy restaurants hit the streets. Revel in a showcase of the nation’s trendsetting culinary masters as well as world-premiere spirits and wines with over 11,000 other visitors.

title: Red, White, and Blue Salute
description: Red, White, and Blue Salute is a special event dedicated to honoring military members and veterans for their service and sacrifice. Guests can enjoy unique Veterans Day-themed entertainment and activities throughout the park, with support from Navy Federal Credit Union. To show appreciation, SeaWorld San Diego offers a dedicated entrance line for military personnel and veterans, making their visit even more memorable. This celebration invites families and the community to come together in recognition of our nation’s heroes.

title: Veterans Day Parade and Festival
description: North County VetFest in Escondido, California, brings the community together to honor the dedication and sacrifices of veterans from World War II to present-day conflicts. The event features a lively parade through the heart of Escondido, with veterans, local organizations, and community groups participating. After the parade, Grape Day Park hosts family-friendly activities, sponsor booths, and opportunities to connect with veterans, followed by a celebration at American Legion Post 149 with a special ceremony, food, and live entertainment. This gathering highlights Escondido’s ongoing commitment to recognizing and supporting those who have served.

title: Coastal Christmas Holiday Lights Spectacular Del Mar
description: Coastal Christmas Holiday Lights Spectacular at the Del Mar Fairgrounds in San Diego, California, invites families and friends to experience a dazzling holiday celebration by the sea. The fairgrounds are transformed into a magical landscape filled with sparkling light displays, festive décor, and seasonal cheer. Visitors can stroll through immersive installations and enjoy a joyful atmosphere perfect for creating holiday memories. This event offers a unique way to celebrate the season in a scenic Southern California setting.

title: A Very Furry Christmas
description: A Very Furry Christmas at Sesame Place San Diego transforms the park into a cheerful holiday wonderland, inviting families to celebrate the season with beloved Sesame Street characters. Guests can enjoy festive shows, holiday-themed attractions, and interactive experiences designed for all ages. The event features dazzling decorations and plenty of opportunities for memorable photos with favorite furry friends. It’s a joyful way to create lasting holiday memories in a playful, welcoming setting.

title: San Diego International Jewish Film Festival
description: Flip the projector switch at the San Diego International Jewish Film Festival in San Diego, CA, where the best Jewish-related films are showcased in many genres to educate and illuminate through independent narrative, evocative, and documentary films. If you can’t wait to view prestigious films, then hurry to the San Diego International Jewish Film Festival.

title: The Kook Run
description: The Kook Run in Encinitas, California, is a celebrated Super Bowl Sunday tradition that draws over 2,500 runners, walkers, and costume enthusiasts to the scenic Coast Highway 101. Participants enjoy breathtaking ocean views and a course that starts with a gentle downhill before looping back through downtown under the iconic “ENCINITAS” sign. The event features a competitive Pro and Elite 10K with a prize purse, a Junior Kook Run for kids, and a lively costume contest.

title: San Diego Bird Festival
description: Grab your binoculars and gaze at the birds during the San Diego Bird Festival. The festival features workshops, keynote speakers, boat trips, bus trips, bird photography programs, as well as mixers, meals, and socials.

title: San Diego Festival of Science and Engineering
description: The San Diego Festival of Science and Engineering in San Diego, California, is a dynamic celebration designed to ignite curiosity in STEAM fields among young learners. The festival’s signature EXPO Day offers hands-on experiments, robotics demos, and live science shows for students from pre-K through high school, drawing tens of thousands of attendees. Specialty zones like the Art Pavilion, Cybersecurity Zone, and Robotics Round-Up provide focused, interactive experiences, while Festival Week extends the excitement with workshops and programs across the region. The event also connects older students with industry leaders and educators, making STEAM learning accessible and inspiring for all.

title: San Diego Race for Autism
description: The San Diego Race for Autism is a lively, superhero-themed event held each year at Balboa Park, San Diego, California, bringing together families and supporters to champion neurodiversity and inclusion. Participants can choose from a chip-timed 5K run, a 5K walk, or a 1-mile Family Walk, making it accessible for all ages and abilities. The event fosters community spirit while raising awareness and support for autism programs in the region. With its welcoming atmosphere and scenic park setting, it’s a memorable way to make a positive impact.

title: WOW Festival
description: The WOW Festival is an annual celebration of creativity, art, and culture that brings together performers, artists, and enthusiasts from all walks of life. With a diverse lineup of live performances, interactive art installations, and thought-provoking workshops, this festival offers something for everyone. Visitors can immerse themselves in a dynamic environment of innovation and inspiration, enjoying everything from music and dance to visual arts. It’s a must-attend event for those looking to experience an eclectic mix of global talent and creativity.

title: Coronado Flower Show
description: The Coronado Flower Show in Coronado, California, is the largest tented flower show in the United States, offering a stunning display of floral beauty and gardening excellence. The event features exquisite floral arrangements, landscape exhibits, and a variety of gardening demonstrations. Visitors can stroll through themed displays, enjoy live entertainment, and explore a marketplace with plants, gardening tools, and artisan goods.
